metadata.description:
# Remote Access & Game Streaming

FreeCompute must support both productivity and gaming workloads.

The platform should be designed to allow users to remotely access virtual machines and hosted systems with low latency.

Supported modes:

- Desktop Mode
- Development Mode
- Gaming Mode
- Remote Support Mode

---

# Remote Support

Users may securely connect to their own machines or approved hosted systems.

Features:

- Browser-based remote desktop
- Clipboard sync
- File transfer
- Multi-monitor support (future)
- Audio forwarding
- Session recording (optional)
- Permission controls
- Temporary access links
- Session timeout controls
- Connection logs

Security:

- End-to-end encrypted connections when possible
- User approval required for remote-control sessions
- Audit logs
- Session expiration
- Device verification

---

# Gaming Mode

Gaming should be treated as a first-class feature.

Goals:

- Low latency
- Hardware acceleration
- GPU scheduling
- Controller support
- Audio streaming
- Full-screen mode
- High refresh rate support

Supported Inputs:

- Keyboard
- Mouse
- Touch
- Xbox controllers
- PlayStation controllers
- Generic gamepads

Future:

- Racing wheel support
- HOTAS support
- VR experimentation

---

# Streaming Architecture

Preferred technologies:

- WebRTC
- H.264
- H.265 (future)
- AV1 (future)

Requirements:

- Adaptive bitrate
- Adaptive resolution
- Network quality detection
- Packet loss recovery
- Latency monitoring

Modes:

Safe Mode
- Maximum compatibility
- Lower resource usage
- More stable

Fast Mode
- Prioritize latency
- Higher resource usage
- Better gaming experience

---

# Session Types

Desktop Session
- Browser
- Terminal
- Development

Gaming Session
- GPU preferred
- Fullscreen
- Controller support

Remote Support Session
- Temporary access
- User authorization
- Logging enabled

Host Session
- Host maintenance tools
- Host diagnostics

---

# Host Requirements

Gaming-capable hosts should report:

- GPU model
- VRAM
- Driver version
- Encoder support
- Available resources
- Network speed

Scheduler should prioritize:

- Lowest latency
- Available GPU
- Network quality
- Current host load

---

# Resource Classes

Basic
- 1 Core
- Shared RAM

Standard
- 2-4 Cores
- More RAM

Gaming
- Dedicated GPU allocation when available

Workstation
- High-performance systems
- Content creation
- Development workloads

---

# Future Features

- Cloud save synchronization
- Persistent game storage
- Save snapshots
- VM snapshots
- LAN tunneling
- Multiplayer optimization
- Steam integration
- Game launcher integration
- Game library management
- User-installed applications
and also, add a proxy which supports all types of connections (app connections from the webOS and the normal browser, https, tcp, http, udp, p2p, webrtc, ssh on the local webos or the browser like ssheasy) and im
